mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate below the estimated neutral rate suppresses short-term discount rates and supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for FEDFUNDS is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, wage pressures begin to compress corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets reprice earnings growth downward.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when UNRATE is below 5.0%, or the Rank IC of UNRATE level is positive and significant (p < 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales and residential construction creates a reinforcing demand impulse that boosts corporate revenue expectations; the joint acceleration of RSAFS and HOUST predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days beyond either series alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST fails to outperform the linear sum of individual effects by at least 2 percentage points annualized over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During the early stage of an industrial production recovery, month-over-month gains in INDPRO signal improving capacity utilization and operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances during identified early-cycle recoveries, or INDPRO changes show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns (t-stat < 1.5) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.5 percent after an extended period near zero, the initiation of a tightening cycle increases the opportunity cost of equity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered observations, or the mean return conditional on FEDFUNDS > 0.5 is not significantly lower than unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in payroll employment growth above its recent trend signals strengthening household income and consumption, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in higher near-term earnings.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when PAYEMS 3-month change exceeds 500, or the hit rate of positive returns falls below 52% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive change in industrial production over the prior three months signals improving manufacturing momentum and operating leverage, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO 3-month change is positive, or the mean conditional return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts during a low interest rate environment signal durable demand strength in interest-sensitive sectors, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as construction multipliers lift broad corporate earnings.
failure_condition: HOUST levels above 1200 are associated with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the conditional mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.
